_cite.reason: 4c: relabel unlabeled workspace-analysis to labeled analysis (o13 §1); every binding sentence judged WORKSPACE_ANALYSIS

prior.content_md:
This document covers the IMO Net-Zero Framework as approved at MEPC 83 (7–11 April 2025) and the current state of its path to formal adoption and entry into force. It is produced for a freight forwarder whose ocean freight activity is tertiary by volume but spans all three primary trade lanes (Americas, Europe, Asia) and services high-value, time-sensitive cargo verticals.

**Convention note:** "Approved" and "adopted" are distinct IMO process stages. MEPC 83 approved the draft amendments; formal adoption — which triggers the MARPOL 16-month entry-into-force clock — has not yet occurred as of this writing. The October 2025 extraordinary session (MEPC ES.2) is reported in the existing brief preview as having adjourned the adoption vote for one year; this document treats that adjournment as context requiring verification against authoritative post-ES.2 reporting before acting on specific timelines.

**Research gap:** The existing brief preview states that MEPC ES.2 (October 2025) adjourned the adoption vote for one year. No source within the available source pool confirms this adjournment with primary-source documentation. The IMO source pool entry (id: 1c2521c0) references the IMO homepage generally. The claim is directionally consistent with reported US opposition but must be confirmed against the official MEPC ES.2 meeting summary before the timeline in Section 14 is treated as final. **Legal Confirmation Required** for timeline reliance.

*Source: IMO Press Briefing, "IMO approves net-zero regulations for global shipping," IMO, 11 April 2025. https://www.imo.org/en/mediacentre/pressbriefings/pages/imo-approves-netzero-regulations.aspx (Tier 1 — binding-process announcement from regulator). 2023 IMO GHG Strategy: Resolution MEPC.377(80), IMO, 7 July 2023. https://wwwcdn.imo.org/localresources/en/KnowledgeCentre/IndexofIMOResolutions/MEPCDocuments/MEPC.377(80).pdf (Tier 1 — IMO resolution).*
